title: Providing accurate information - review rights and chance to comment

sections.heading: Chance to comment
sections.section_ref: Home Affairs guidance / Chance to comment
sections.text: Department of Home Affairs guidance states that if the Department is not satisfied about identity, believes bogus documents or false or misleading information has been provided, or considers a non-grant period issue may apply, it will notify the applicant and invite comments within a specified time. This is useful for intake triage because the client should be asked whether they received such a notice and whether they responded on time.

sections.heading: Review rights after refusal
sections.text: The Department states that if a visa application is refused, the applicant may have a right to review by the Administrative Review Tribunal and that the refusal notice will advise the person of any available review rights. For consultation preparation, the refusal notice itself is therefore a key source document.

sections.heading: Future applications after certain refusals
sections.section_ref: Home Affairs guidance / Future visa applications
sections.text: The same guidance notes that where an application is refused under PIC 4020, future application restrictions may apply, especially if the person remains in Australia without holding a substantive visa. This means intake should capture whether the refusal involved identity, bogus documents, false information, or PIC 4020-related concerns.
